it:21.2: This was said by the Blessed One, said by the Arahant, so I have heard:
it:21.3: "There is the case where a certain person is clear-minded. Having encompassed that mind with [my] awareness, I discern, 'If this person were to die at this instant, then as if he were to be carried off, he would thus be placed in heaven.' Why is that? Because his mind is clear. It's because of clear-mindedness that there are cases where beings — at the break-up of the body, after death — reappear in the heavenly world."
it:21.4: Knowing the case of a clear-minded person, the One Awakened explained its meaning in the presence of the monks.
it:21.5: If that person were to die at this instant, he'd reappear in heaven because his mind is clear —
it:21.6: As if he were carried off and placed there. It's because of clear-mindedness that beings go to a good destination.
it:21.7: This, too, was the meaning of what was said by the Blessed One, so I have heard.
